footer.about: Velxio is a free, open-source circuit and microcontroller simulator that runs entirely in your browser. Arduino, ESP32, RP2040 and ATtiny85, with SPICE-accurate analog co-simulation. Built for makers, students and embedded engineers learning by doing. AGPLv3.

hero.subtitle: Write code, wire up components, hit run. 19 boards, an AI agent that builds circuits for you, real CPU emulation, full offline desktop app, AGPLv3 open source.
hero.trustLine: AI agent included · Works offline · Open source AGPLv3 · No signup needed
hero.imageAlt: Velxio editor with Arduino Uno traffic-light circuit

boards.label: Supported Hardware
boards.subtitle: 19 boards across 5 CPU architectures — AVR8, ARM Cortex-M0+, RISC-V, Xtensa, and Linux. All running locally, no cloud needed.

features.label: Features
features.title: Everything you need.
features.subtitle: A complete IDE, compiler, and multi-architecture simulator — running locally with no external services required.

spice.title: Real-Time SPICE Analog
spice.desc: ngspice-WASM engine — full Modified Nodal Analysis at ~60 Hz. Resistors, capacitors, op-amps, BJTs, MOSFETs, regulators behave like real silicon, not idealised models.

engines.title: 5 Emulation Engines
engines.desc: AVR8 (ATmega328P, ATmega2560, ATtiny85), RP2040 (ARM Cortex-M0+), RV32IMC (ESP32-C3, CH32V003 via QEMU), Xtensa LX6/LX7 (ESP32 via QEMU), and ARM Cortex-A53 (Raspberry Pi 3 Linux).

customChips.title: Custom Chips
customChips.desc: Author your own integrated circuits in C, Rust, or AssemblyScript using the Wokwi Custom Chips API. Compile to WebAssembly and reuse across projects.

components.title: 100+ Components
components.desc: SPICE-accurate analog parts plus 48+ wokwi visual components — LEDs, LCDs, ILI9341 TFT, servos, buzzers, ultrasonic sensors, keypads, NeoPixel strips.

instruments.title: Live Instruments
instruments.desc: Multi-channel oscilloscope, voltmeter, ammeter, signal generator. Drop on any node and watch waveforms update in real time as the simulation runs.

monaco.title: Monaco Editor + arduino-cli
monaco.desc: VS Code-grade C++ editor with multi-file workspace. Local arduino-cli compiles to real .hex / .uf2 / .bin files — no cloud round-trip.

support.title: Support the project
support.subtitle: Velxio is free and open source. If it saves you time, consider supporting its development.

licensing.label: Licensing
licensing.title: Free, with a commercial option.
licensing.subtitle: AGPLv3 for the open-source release. A commercial license for teams that need to ship Velxio inside closed-source products.

opensource.title: Open source — AGPLv3
opensource.desc: Free forever for personal use, education, research and self-hosting. Modify, fork and redistribute — your changes stay under AGPLv3 too.

commercial.title: Commercial license
commercial.desc: For closed-source products, proprietary SaaS, or internal company use where AGPLv3 reciprocity is incompatible with your business model.

ai.label: AI agent
ai.title: Powered by AI agents.
ai.subtitle: A multi-step agent runs inside the editor. It reads your canvas, writes code, places parts and wires them up — all driven by natural language. Backed by frontier LLMs auto-routed for cost and reliability.

wire.title: Drop & wire components
wire.desc: Ask "add a button on D2 with a pull-up to 5V". The agent picks the parts, drops them on the canvas, connects every pin and labels the wires.

code.title: Generate working sketches
code.desc: Describe what you want the board to do and the agent writes the Arduino / MicroPython code, compiles it, and loads the firmware — no copy-paste from the chat.

debug.title: Diagnose live circuits
debug.desc: Stuck on a wiring bug? Share the canvas screenshot and the agent reads the schematic, runs a sim, and tells you which pin is wrong.

pricing.label: Pricing
pricing.title: Free to run circuits. Pay for the AI.
pricing.subtitle: The simulator stays free forever. Plans only raise the AI agent quota — credits roll over each day.

free.name: Free
free.tagline: Discover the simulator + light AI help.

maker.name: Maker
maker.tagline: For makers shipping real circuits with AI help.
maker.f3: Unlimited ESP32, STM32 & Raspberry Pi run time

pro.name: Pro
pro.tagline: Private projects, GitHub Sync and BOM exports.
pro.f2: Private projects + BOM CSV + schematic PNG

pricing.classroomBanner: Bringing Velxio into a course? Velxio for Classroom gives every student Pro access from $40/year.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.selfHosted: This Velxio instance is self-hosted from the open-source image — all features are free for everyone using it.
pricing.hostedVersion: The hosted version at <link>velxio.dev</link> offers an optional Pro tier that helps fund development.

starBanner.title: Enjoying Velxio?
starBanner.body: A ⭐ on GitHub helps more developers discover the project — it only takes 2 seconds!
starBanner.body2: Velxio is free and open-source. A star on GitHub is the simplest way to support it and help more people find the project.

errors.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters.

quotaModal.bodyFree: Free accounts get {{cap}} AI messages a day. Pro ($15/mo) gives you {{proCap}}/day — {{multiplier}}× the cap. Or wait until your quota resets.
quotaModal.bodyPaid: Your {{plan}} plan allows {{cap}} messages per day. Upgrade to a higher tier or wait for the reset.

share.updateFailed: Could not update visibility — please try again.

visibility.publicHint: Anyone can find this project on velxio.dev.
visibility.unlistedHint: Hidden from search and the gallery — only accessible via the share link.
visibility.privateHint: Only you can see this project.

agent.title: AI Assistant
agent.settingsHint: Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint works. Fields left blank fall back to the server's defaults. Everything is stored only in this browser.
agent.needKey: No API key yet — <1>open endpoint settings</1> and enter your OpenAI-compatible base URL and key (stored only in this browser).
agent.emptyTitle: Describe a project in plain language — the AI builds the circuit, wiring and code, then compiles and runs it.
agent.emptySub: You can hand-edit anything afterwards; the AI re-reads the latest state every turn.
agent.restore: Restore project to before this message
agent.placeholder: Describe what you want to build, e.g. a breathing LED
agent.hint: Enter to send · Shift+Enter for newline
agent.restoreConfirm: Restore the whole project (circuit + code) to the state before this message? Unsaved changes will be lost.
agent.steerPlaceholder: Type to steer the agent while it works…
agent.capped: The agent paused at the step limit — the task may be unfinished.
agent.compacted: Earlier context was compacted into a summary

cloud.name: Display name
cloud.selfHostNote: Accounts live on THIS server only (self-hosted SQLite). The admin of this instance can see stored data — don't reuse an important password.
cloud.loadConfirm: Open this cloud project? Unsaved changes in the current workspace will be lost.
cloud.deleteConfirm: Delete "{{name}}" from the cloud? This cannot be undone.
cloud.loadChatConfirm: Open this conversation? The current chat panel content will be replaced (it stays saved in the cloud).
cloud.deleteChatConfirm: Delete this conversation from the cloud?
cloud.syncError: Sync failed — will retry on next change
cloud.signInForHistory: Sign in to keep your conversations in the cloud and access them from any device.
cloud.teacherCode: Teacher registration code (leave blank if your school has none)

versions.title: Version history
versions.unchanged: Nothing new to save — identical to the newest version
versions.empty: No versions yet. The AI saves one before every turn; you can also save one here.
versions.restoreConfirm: Restore the project to "{{label}}"? Current state is backed up automatically first.
versions.restored: Restored "{{label}}". A backup of the previous state was saved.
versions.restoreFailed: Restore failed — the version could not be loaded.
versions.deleteConfirm: Delete version "{{label}}"? This cannot be undone.
versions.hint: 🖐 manual · ✨ saved by AI turn · ⟳ automatic backup. Up to 50 versions kept; manual saves are never pruned.

brand.name: AI IoT Lab
brand.seoTitle: AI IoT Lab — Free Online Arduino / ESP32 Simulator & IoT Course Platform
brand.seoDescription: A free, open-source learning platform for middle/high-school students and teachers: simulate Arduino, ESP32 and 19 boards in the browser, with interactive courses, quizzes, class management and an AI teaching assistant.

hero.badge: Free · Open source · No install · No hardware
hero.subtitle: Write code, wire circuits, run simulations — watch Arduino and ESP32 come alive. Learn step by step with interactive courses, ask the AI tutor anytime, and let teachers track the whole class.

sim.title: Real simulator
sim.desc: Not an animation — your code really runs: chip-level AVR, ESP32 and RP2040 emulation, 100+ electronic components, oscilloscope and serial monitor, behaving just like real hardware.

courses.title: Interactive courses
courses.desc: From lighting your first LED to putting an ESP32 online: every lesson has clear theory, a one-click circuit example, and a hands-on challenge — learn by doing.

quiz.title: Quizzes & classes
quiz.desc: Each lesson ends with a multiple-choice quiz with instant feedback and auto-grading; teachers create a class code, students join in one click, and the dashboard shows live progress and scores.

ai.title: An AI tutor, always by your side
ai.desc: Say "build me a temperature alarm" and the AI tutor places components, wires them, writes the firmware and runs the simulation on your canvas; when you are stuck, ask why — it explains one concept at a time, like a patient teacher.
ai.demoUser: Why does my LED not light up?
ai.demoAi: I checked your circuit: the LED cathode is wired to D13 — it should go to GND to close the loop. Want me to fix it and show you?

teacherSection.title: For teachers: a ready-to-use computer classroom
teacherSection.desc: No hardware to buy, nothing to install: open a class code and students complete a whole semester of experiments in the browser. Progress and quiz scores at a glance — and you can self-host on a school server, keeping all data yours.

footer.oss: Built on the open-source Velxio project, licensed AGPLv3

learn.seoDescription: Interactive Arduino and ESP32 IoT courses: theory, one-click circuit examples, hands-on challenges and quizzes.
learn.title: Courses
learn.subtitle: Every lesson is hands-on, right in your browser: read the theory, open the circuit example, complete the challenge, then check yourself with a short quiz.
learn.signinHint: Progress is stored on this computer for now. Sign in to sync across devices and let your teacher see your progress.

class.unknownCode: Unknown class code — please check with your teacher.
class.joinFailed: Could not join, please try again later.

teacher.seoDescription: Create classes, hand out join codes, and track every student's lesson progress and quiz scores in real time.
teacher.title: Teaching
teacher.needSignin: Sign in with a teacher account to create and manage classes.
teacher.needTeacherRole: This page needs a teacher account. You are currently a student — if you are a teacher, register a new account with the Teacher role.
teacher.namePlaceholder: Class name, e.g. Grade 8 Class A
teacher.empty: No classes yet. Create one, hand the class code to your students, and they can join from the Courses page.
teacher.deleteConfirm: Delete this class? Students keep their personal progress, but the class and its roster will be removed.
teacher.noMembers: No students yet. Share the code {{code}} with your class!
